label: Value of Travel Time Savings (ATAP methodology)
description: Australian VTTS is derived as a percentage of Average Weekly Earnings (AWE), not from stated preference surveys as in the UK. Values update automatically as AWE changes.

private_travel.label: Private (non-business) travel time
private_travel.note: 40% of AWE per hour. Covers commuting and leisure.

business_travel.label: Business travel time
business_travel.note: 129.8% of AWE per hour. Includes employer on-costs (superannuation, workers comp, payroll tax).

walking_waiting.label: Walking and waiting time
walking_waiting.note: Valued at 2x the private travel rate (perceived as more onerous)

reference_awe.note: ABS 6302.0 Average Weekly Earnings, full-time adult total earnings, seasonally adjusted. Hourly = AWE / 38.

computed_vtts.description: Current VTTS based on reference AWE above

car_per_km.note: ATAP PV2 average across vehicle classes. Includes fuel, tyres, maintenance, depreciation.

source.publication: ATAP PV2: Road Parameter Values; ATAP T2: Travel Time
source.update_frequency: periodic (values update with AWE releases, twice yearly)

caveats: VTTS updates automatically with AWE. Always use the latest ABS 6302.0 release.
Formula-based approach means no stated preference uncertainty, but assumes a fixed relationship between earnings and time value.
Walking/waiting multiplier of 2x is an assumption, not empirically derived for Australia.
